errorCode.title: GMV protection alarm H5
errorCode.meaning: Preliminary diagnostic entry for a Gree GMV H5 alarm. Verify the exact meaning against the service manual for the installed model before concluding.

likelyCauses: Outdoor unit protection condition reported by the control board.

diagnosticSteps.title: Confirm installed equipment identity
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Record the outdoor unit model, GMV series, serial plate data, and controller-displayed error code.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Manufacturer, series, model, and displayed H5 code are confirmed before diagnosis continues.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Stop classification and obtain the exact model information.

requiredMeasurements.name: Supply voltage
requiredMeasurements.description: Measure the supply voltage at the equipment terminals using approved service procedures.

safetyNotes: Electrical, compressor, inverter, refrigerant, and chiller protection checks must be performed by a qualified technician.

source.notes: Seeded deterministic diagnostic guidance. Not manually page-verified.
limitations: Use as preliminary diagnostic guidance only.
Verify against the exact service manual for the installed equipment family before final conclusion.

shortSummary: Gree GMV H5: GMV protection alarm H5. Preliminary diagnostic entry for a Gree GMV H5 alarm. Verify the exact meaning against the service manual for the installed model before concluding.
recommendedNextChecks: Step 1: Confirm installed equipment identity - Record the outdoor unit model, GMV series, serial plate data, and controller-displayed error code.
Step 2: Check electrical supply condition - A qualified technician should measure supply voltage and phase condition at the service point using approved procedures.
Step 3: Inspect protection-related wiring - Inspect compressor, inverter, control-board, and protection-sensor connections for visible damage, loose terminals, or water ingress.
Record Supply voltage (V) before drawing a conclusion.
Record Operating current (A) before drawing a conclusion.
confidenceExplanation: Low confidence seeded guidance: use as preliminary diagnostic support only and verify the exact installed model, controller, and service manual before drawing a conclusion.

applicabilitySummary: Applicable series: GMV; no specific applicable models listed. Limitations: Use as preliminary diagnostic guidance only. Verify against the exact service manual for the installed equipment family before final conclusion.
safetyBoundary: VRF outdoor electrical, compressor, inverter, refrigerant, and protection checks must stay within qualified-technician service scope. Catalog safety note: Electrical, compressor, inverter, refrigerant, and chiller protection checks must be performed by a qualified technician.
